title: 150+ Roles — World's #1 Voice AI Company
description: ElevenLabs is the world's leading AI voice platform ($3.3B valuation), offering 150+ open roles across engineering, growth, revenue, and operations — plus separate freelance roles for audiobook specialists, dubbing specialists, translators, and audio engineers. India-specific roles are available. This is a rare platform offering both full-time careers AND paid freelance work at the same company.
good_fit_for: Engineers, marketers, and sales professionals for full-time roles; linguists, translators, and audio specialists for the freelance productions path
typically_needs: Full-time roles need relevant experience; freelance roles (dubbing, transcription, audiobook) depend on language skills and audio quality
keep_in_mind: Most full-time roles are remote but tied to specific countries — check your region. Freelance productions roles are available across Brazil, UK, India and more

title: PhD & Expert Network — Teams Not Crowds
description: Scholars by iMerit is an expert network for PhDs, MDs, linguists and engineers who do AI training and chain-of-thought reasoning work for LLM fine-tuning. Unlike anonymous gig platforms, iMerit puts you on a small team with a project manager rather than treating you as part of a crowd — backed by 12+ years of company history.
good_fit_for: Master's/PhD holders and subject matter experts across professional, academic, and creative fields — from medicine to mathematics to creative writing
typically_needs: A relevant advanced degree (MA, MSC, or PhD depending on the project) and proficiency in the required language for that role
keep_in_mind: Projects range from a few days to long-term contracts, and some listings are "pre-register" only — meaning you apply ahead of the actual project start date

title: 50+ Remote Corporate Roles — 20+ Countries
company_line: AI Acquisition LLC · Marketing, Ops, Customer Success
description: AI Acquisition is a marketing/growth agency that sells AI-powered business systems to entrepreneurs — NOT an "earn from AI" platform. Their careers page lists 50+ genuine internal job openings (Marketing, Operations, Customer Success, Finance) across 20+ countries including India. Treat this as a normal remote job application, not a gig-economy opportunity.
good_fit_for: Marketing, Operations, or Customer Success professionals looking for standard full-time or contract remote employment
typically_needs: Relevant experience matching the specific department — these are standard corporate hires with normal interview processes
keep_in_mind: This company's main business is selling AI marketing systems to entrepreneurs, with bold revenue claims that come with heavy disclaimers — but the job openings themselves appear to be real, standard hiring

title: Full-Time Careers OR Expert Community
company_line: Snorkel AI · SOC2 & HIPAA Certified · 30+ Roles
description: Snorkel AI offers two genuinely different paths: full-time corporate roles in Engineering, Research, Sales and Product (mostly based in San Francisco/Redwood City, hybrid), and a separate Expert Community for PhDs and domain specialists who want contract-based, remote-friendly work building AI training datasets. Pick whichever matches what you're looking for.
good_fit_for: Tech professionals wanting full-time SF-based careers, OR PhDs/specialists wanting flexible remote contract work — two very different audiences
typically_needs: Full-time roles need relevant tech/research experience; Expert Community needs deep expertise in a specialized field, any background
keep_in_mind: Most full-time listings are hybrid in SF/Redwood City/NYC — true remote is mainly available through the Expert Community path or select "United States (Remote)" roles

title: Global Tax-Tech Company — Multiple Roles
description: Avalara is a publicly-traded (NYSE: AVLR) tax compliance and AI platform with 200,000+ customers across 75+ countries. This is a stable, traditional corporate job rather than a freelance gig — Glassdoor shows a 3.2/5 employee rating with 49% recommending it to a friend, which is solid but not exceptional. Open roles include account management, benefits systems, and engineering positions, with some based in Pune, India.
good_fit_for: Professionals wanting stable, long-term corporate employment rather than quick freelance income
typically_needs: Relevant degree and work experience matching the specific role — these are standard full-time hires, not gig work
keep_in_mind: Customer reviews of Avalara's tax product mention support issues — this reflects the product experience, not necessarily what it's like to work there as an employee (3.2/5 on Glassdoor)

title: Get Paid to Record Your Voice for AI
description: Besimple AI is a Y Combinator-backed startup building the data layer for voice AI. Their contributor side, Pila8, pays everyday people (not just engineers) to record short voice clips that train AI to understand different accents and languages — base rate is $0.10/minute, with non-English speakers and consistent contributors earning bonuses.
good_fit_for: Anyone with a phone, a quiet space, and a few minutes a day — non-English speakers get higher bonus rates
typically_needs: A Google account to sign up — no technical skills, just read provided scripts and record clearly
keep_in_mind: There's a 20-day maturity lock before you can withdraw, and you're granting rights to your voice (and face, for video) data — read the agreement first

title: 1.3M Worldwide Community — Sign Up in 5 Min
description: DataForce is TransPerfect's AI data platform with over 1.3 million remote contributors worldwide, working on data annotation, transcription, search evaluation, and AI training tasks. It's a legitimate, established side of TransPerfect (25+ years, 140+ cities) — but reviews say to treat it as flexible freelance income, not a stable full-time job, since project availability varies by location and language demand.
good_fit_for: Bilingual speakers, students, and anyone wanting flexible side income — beginners welcome, no AI experience needed
typically_needs: A 5-minute sign-up plus a qualification step — pay is calculated per task, per hour, or flat rate depending on the project
keep_in_mind: Some users report waiting weeks for project access — approval timing depends heavily on your location and language demand

title: 622 Open Positions — Hiring Now!
description: Revolut is a global fintech "super app" with 60+ million customers and 622 open roles across Engineering, Compliance, Sales, Finance, and Legal. It's genuinely remote-first — 239 of its open roles are fully remote — and known for fast growth, strong pay (engineers average $126K+), and an intense, results-driven culture rather than a relaxed one.
good_fit_for: Experienced professionals in fintech, compliance, engineering, or finance who want high pay and don't mind a fast, demanding pace
typically_needs: Relevant degree or work experience for the specific team — most roles are full-time employment, not freelance/gig work
keep_in_mind: Reviews mention long hours (12–14 hrs some days) and around 500 applicants per role — competitive, demanding, but well-compensated

title: 25+ Years Global Excellence — Hiring
company_line: Welo Global · Google, Spotify & Amazon Trusted
description: Welo Global is a 25+ year old language and localization company trusted by Google, Spotify, Amazon, and other major brands — they handle translation, transcription, data annotation, and AI training tasks across 300+ languages. It's one of the more established names in this space, which generally means more structured onboarding and steadier project flow compared to smaller gig platforms.
good_fit_for: Bilingual or multilingual speakers, especially those with skills in less common languages — but general annotators are also welcome
typically_needs: A profile sign-up and language proficiency assessment — role type and pay depend on the specific project you qualify for
keep_in_mind: "Multiple" roles means pay varies a lot by task — check the specific project type after onboarding rather than assuming a single rate

title: Earn $25/hr+ Training AI — No Experience
description: Stellar AI is a freelance AI training platform open to contributors worldwide with no prior experience required — you complete tasks like rating responses, writing prompts, or evaluating AI outputs, and get paid hourly. The "$25/hr+" framing means that's the starting rate, with higher-paying tasks available depending on skill and task type. Being "open-ended contract" means there's no fixed end date, but also no guaranteed hours — work is available when projects are live.
good_fit_for: Beginners looking for a low-barrier entry into AI training work — no degree or prior experience needed to start
typically_needs: Just a sign-up and a short onboarding — most tasks are self-explanatory with built-in guidelines
keep_in_mind: Hours aren't guaranteed — task availability depends on active projects, so treat this as flexible side income rather than a fixed schedule

title: World's Largest All-Remote Company
description: GitLab is a major, publicly traded (Nasdaq: GTLB) DevOps platform company that's run fully remote since it was founded — no offices, no headquarters, just a globally distributed team across 65+ countries. It's a different kind of listing than most others here: this is full-time employment at an established public tech company, not freelance or gig-based AI training work.
good_fit_for: People wanting a stable, full-time remote job at an established public company — not freelance gig work
typically_needs: A standard full-time hiring process — resume screening, interviews, and role-specific assessments
keep_in_mind: Roles span engineering, sales, marketing and more — check GitLab's own careers page to filter by your actual skill set

title: Top 0.01% Expert Network — All Domains
description: Pareto AI runs a human-data platform that connects AI labs with vetted subject-matter experts for RLHF, prompt evaluation, and domain-specific annotation — across fields like medicine, law, finance, and engineering. It sits at the high end of this list: the company says general work pays $35–$60/hr, with specialized expert tasks going up to $230/hr, and that it has raised $37M in funding so far.
good_fit_for: Licensed or credentialed specialists — doctors, lawyers, engineers, quants — not just general annotation experience
typically_needs: A completed profile, a paid screening task, and proof of your professional background
keep_in_mind: $230/hr applies to specialized expert work, not the general rate — check which tier you actually qualify for before applying

title: Expert-First AI Training — All Domains
description: Rex.zone connects subject-matter experts with AI labs that need domain-specific data — model answers get checked, scored, or rewritten across fields like law, medicine, finance and more. Work is task-based and remote, so you pick projects that match what you actually know.
good_fit_for: People with deep knowledge in a specific field, not just general writing skill
typically_needs: A laptop, stable internet, and proof of expertise (resume or portfolio) in your domain
keep_in_mind: Apply with the domain you're strongest in — niche expertise usually gets matched to better-paying projects

title: Earn Up to $100/hr — PhDs & Masters
description: Built on Handshake's existing university network, this fellowship targets PhD and Master's-level fellows who evaluate or generate advanced AI responses in their academic specialty. It's one of the higher-paying options here, but it leans heavily on formal qualifications.
good_fit_for: Current or recent grad students with a Master's or PhD
typically_needs: Academic transcripts or proof of degree, often a short subject test
keep_in_mind: Have your degree or transcript ready before applying — it speeds up screening a lot

title: Robotics ML Expert
description: Alignerr recruits ML experts to label and validate robotics-related training data. Worth flagging clearly: the $1000 shown on this card is a referral bonus for bringing in other qualified experts, not the hourly pay for the work itself — actual task pay is set separately once you're onboarded.
good_fit_for: People with a robotics, ML, or computer-vision background
typically_needs: Relevant technical background; the referral bonus needs a successful invite
keep_in_mind: Don't apply expecting $1000/hr — check the real task rate on the application page first

title: Freelance AI Contributor
description: Listed as a flexible freelance contributor role. We don't have much first-hand detail on this one yet, so treat it like any unfamiliar gig listing: read the actual offer page closely before committing real time to it.
good_fit_for: People comfortable evaluating a new platform on their own
typically_needs: Details vary by role — check the official application page
keep_in_mind: Search the company name plus "review" before applying to any platform you haven't heard of

title: AI Expert — 40+ Roles Open
description: AfterQuery is a YC-backed startup hiring AI experts to evaluate and refine model outputs across 40+ open roles. Being YC-backed means it went through outside investor vetting, which is a decent — though not guaranteed — signal of legitimacy.
good_fit_for: Freelancers comfortable with structured evaluation and grading tasks
typically_needs: A remote setup; some roles want a specific technical or writing background
keep_in_mind: Browse a few of the 40+ roles — pay and requirements differ a lot between them

title: AI Trainer — All Skills Welcome
description: Mindrift runs AI training projects across many skill areas and languages, with pay scaling from entry-level to expert tasks. It's a broad, multi-skill platform rather than one narrow niche, so most backgrounds can usually find something to apply for.
good_fit_for: Beginners and experienced contributors alike — roles span a wide pay range
typically_needs: Sign-up plus a short skills assessment for your chosen track
keep_in_mind: Try a couple of different task categories — pay varies more by task type than by platform

title: AI Data Annotator
description: One of the more established platforms on this list — contributors complete coding, writing, and data-labeling tasks that help train AI models, paid hourly. Getting in usually means passing a qualification exercise first; pay tends to rise as you build a track record.
good_fit_for: People willing to start with simpler tasks and build up from there
typically_needs: Passing an initial qualification test before you get access to paid tasks
keep_in_mind: Early tasks pay less while you're being evaluated — that's normal, not a red flag

title: AI Contributor — 60+ Languages
description: OneForma focuses on linguistic and data-collection microtasks — translation checks, transcription, search relevance — across 60+ languages. Pay per task is usually modest since tasks are short, but it's accessible if your language isn't widely supported elsewhere.
good_fit_for: Bilingual or multilingual speakers, especially in less common languages
typically_needs: Native or fluent-level skill in your listed language(s)
keep_in_mind: Multilingual applicants usually get matched to more available tasks, faster

title: AI Expert — 61+ Roles Open
description: Outlier runs expert evaluation tasks — reviewing, scoring, and correcting AI-generated answers — across 60+ open roles in subjects like coding, math, and writing. Pay is task-based rather than a flat hourly rate, so it depends on which project you qualify for.
good_fit_for: People strong in a specific subject — coding, writing, math, and similar
typically_needs: Passing a subject-specific qualification quiz to unlock paid tasks
keep_in_mind: Pick the subject category you're genuinely confident in — qualification tests can be strict

title: Prompt Engineer
description: A part-time Prompt Engineer role — writing and refining prompts used to test or steer AI model behavior. It's narrower and more specialized than most listings here, and the pay reflects a part-time rather than full freelance commitment.
good_fit_for: People comfortable writing clear, structured instructions for AI systems
typically_needs: Strong written English and some familiarity with how AI chat tools respond to prompts
keep_in_mind: A short portfolio of example prompts can help a lot during screening

title: AI Contributor — 500+ Languages
description: Appen is a long-running, publicly listed data company, and CrowdGen is its crowdsourced worker portal for things like search evaluation, data collection, and transcription in 200+ countries. It's a high-volume microtask model — individual tasks pay modestly but availability is broad.
good_fit_for: Anyone wanting flexible, low-commitment microtasks on the side
typically_needs: Just a sign-up and basic task tutorials — no special qualification usually needed
keep_in_mind: Treat this as supplemental income rather than a primary one — task volume can be inconsistent

title: AI Contributor — 7 Roles Open
description: TELUS Digital's AI data arm runs annotation and labeling projects worldwide, currently with seven open contributor roles. Being part of a large, established company, the process tends to be more structured than smaller gig platforms.
good_fit_for: People who prefer a more corporate, process-driven application experience
typically_needs: ID verification or a background check is common for larger BPO-style platforms
keep_in_mind: Expect a slightly longer onboarding process than you'd see on smaller startups

title: Premium AI Jobs — All Skills
description: Mercor matches experts directly with frontier AI labs for premium-paying contract work, with pay varying a lot by skill level and project. It's one of the higher-end options on this list, which usually also means more competitive screening.
good_fit_for: Experienced professionals — engineering, research, or specialized domains
typically_needs: A strong resume or portfolio — screening is more competitive at this pay tier
keep_in_mind: Tailor your application to one specific skill area rather than applying broadly

title: 25+ Remote Tech Jobs Open
description: Listed as full-time remote tech roles rather than freelance gig work, with 25+ positions open. Full-time listings are worth a closer look at company background, since the commitment level here is higher than a freelance task platform.
good_fit_for: People looking for a full-time remote tech job, not freelance gigs
typically_needs: A standard full-time hiring process — interviews, background checks, and so on
keep_in_mind: For any full-time offer, confirm the legal entity and contract terms before signing anything

title: Top 1% AI Expert Network
description: An invite-only network for top-tier AI experts. Invite-only models usually mean a higher entry bar and limited slots, so this one's less about applying on demand and more about getting noticed by the network in the first place.
good_fit_for: Established experts with a public track record — publications, GitHub, portfolio
typically_needs: Typically a referral or an inbound invite rather than an open application
keep_in_mind: A visible public portfolio helps — invite-only networks often source candidates that way

title: Subject Matter Expert — AI Training
description: SuperAnnotate's subject-matter-expert track pays a wide range depending on how specialized your knowledge is, with roles open across 40+ countries. The wide $20–$130+/hr band reflects how much subject expertise can change your rate.
good_fit_for: Specialists in a technical or academic field, not generalists
typically_needs: Proof of subject expertise — degree, certification, or relevant work history
keep_in_mind: The high end of that pay range usually goes to rare or highly technical specialties

title: LLM Trainer — Agent Function Call
description: Turing places software and AI engineers with major companies on a project basis, including LLM-training work like agent function-calling — teaching a model to correctly trigger tools and APIs. Engagements are typically several weeks long rather than one-off tasks.
good_fit_for: Software engineers comfortable with technical interviews
typically_needs: Usually a coding assessment plus a technical interview
keep_in_mind: Brush up on tool-use and function-calling patterns specifically before the interview

title: Language & Audio Remote Jobs
description: Focused specifically on language and audio work — transcription, voice recording, pronunciation review for AI speech models. It's a narrower niche than most listings here, so it suits people with strong audio or language skills more than general data work.
good_fit_for: People with clear speech, strong language skills, or audio-editing experience
typically_needs: A decent microphone or quiet recording space for audio-based tasks
keep_in_mind: Test your mic setup before any audio task — quality often affects payment approval

title: AI Expert — Frontier Lab Projects
description: Similar in model to Mercor and Turing — micro1 matches AI experts with frontier lab projects, with pay scaling by skill level. A few platforms on this page use this same "staffing agency for AI labs" approach, so requirements tend to overlap.
good_fit_for: Experienced AI/ML practitioners comfortable with a formal screening process
typically_needs: A resume review plus a technical assessment
keep_in_mind: Rejected by one staffing-style platform? The others here (Mercor, Turing) are worth trying too — criteria differ slightly

title: AI Data Specialist — TrainAI Community
description: RWS is an established language-services company expanding into AI training data work through TrainAI, covering translation-adjacent and linguistic annotation tasks. Pay here sits on the lower end of this list, which is typical for high-volume linguistic microtasks.
good_fit_for: Translators or bilingual freelancers looking for AI-adjacent work
typically_needs: Language-pair fluency relevant to the open tasks
keep_in_mind: Useful as a side platform alongside others on this list rather than a sole income source

title: 219 Remote Jobs — Train GROK AI
description: xAI is running a large recruiting push — 219 listed roles — for people who help train and evaluate Grok. A drive at this scale usually means varied roles at very different pay levels, so "up to $200/hr" applies to specific senior tasks, not every opening.
good_fit_for: A wide range of backgrounds, given how many different roles are open
typically_needs: Varies a lot by specific role — check each listing individually
keep_in_mind: Don't assume every one of the 219 roles pays the headline $200/hr — check the actual listing

title: Work for Google, Apple & Microsoft!
description: Innodata is a publicly traded (Nasdaq) AI data engineering company that's been operating for decades, working with major tech clients. As a listed public company, it has more public financial transparency than most platforms on this page.
good_fit_for: People who prefer working with an established, publicly accountable company
typically_needs: A standard full-time application process, often with role-specific technical screening
keep_in_mind: You can check Innodata's public filings or investor info for extra confidence before applying

title: Get Paid for Online Studies
description: Prolific is a bit different from the rest of this list — instead of AI-labeling tasks, it connects you with paid academic and commercial research studies and surveys, some of which feed into AI/UX research. Pay is per-study rather than ongoing hourly task work.
good_fit_for: People who enjoy surveys and short studies rather than ongoing task work
typically_needs: Just a participant profile — no special qualification needed to start
keep_in_mind: Studies fill up fast — turning on notifications helps you catch ones in your demographic

title: 80% of World's AI Models Trained Here!
description: Invisible Technologies runs the Meridial Expert Network, an operations-and-data partner for several major AI labs, with a large existing pool of experts already onboarded. Being part of a bigger network can mean steadier project flow, but also more competition to get matched.
good_fit_for: Generalists comfortable with varied operational and data tasks, not just one niche
typically_needs: Onboarding into the expert network before tasks become available
keep_in_mind: Expect an onboarding period before consistent project flow starts

title: $10K–$1M/Year — Top 1% Remote Jobs
description: Crossover has run remote, performance-based hiring for years, placing people in roles with Fortune 500 clients across 100 countries, with a wide pay band reflecting very different role levels. The "top 1%" framing means the screening process is intentionally selective.
good_fit_for: Highly experienced professionals comfortable with rigorous, performance-tracked work
typically_needs: Multi-stage interviews and skills testing — the process is longer than most
keep_in_mind: Block out real time for the application — Crossover's process is known for being thorough

title: Powers 90% of World's Top AI Models
company_line: Scale AI · Trusted by Meta, Mayo Clinic, US Gov
description: Scale AI is one of the most established names in AI training data, founded in 2016 and used by many major AI labs. As the most well-known platform on this list, expect higher applicant volume and correspondingly more competitive task qualification.
good_fit_for: Anyone wanting to start with a recognizable, widely-used platform
typically_needs: Account sign-up plus task-specific qualification tests
keep_in_mind: Popular task categories fill fast — check back regularly for new openings
